description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in V8's WasmInternalFunction GC body descriptor. WasmInternalFunction::BodyDescriptor::IterateBody() in src/objects/objects-body-descriptors-inl.h does not call IterateCodePointer for the 'code' field (an IndirectPointer<Code> at kCodeOffset = kEndOfStrongFieldsOffset, outside the iterated strong fields range [kStartOfStrongFieldsOffset, kEndOfStrongFieldsOffset)). When --jit-fuzzing sets wasm_wrapper_tiering_budget=1, the first call to a WebAssembly.Function wrapping a JavaScript function triggers TierUpWasmToJSWrapper, which installs a freshly-compiled Code object into WasmInternalFunction.code and resets call_target to 0. If the JavaScript function then triggers GC (e.g., via gc()), the GC cannot trace the newly-installed Code object through the code field due to the missing IterateCodePointer call, causing the Code object to be collected. On the next call_ref, CallRefIC reads the freed code pointer table entry (containing kFreeCodePointerTableEntryTag | freelist_index = 0xffff00000000200e) and uses it as the call target, causing a SIGSEGV.

fixes.evidence_text: Local patch generated from verified V8 commit b93975a48c722c2e5fe9b39437738eb2e23dac74 for CL 5374907.
